issues.body: The `marketing` variant displays `constraint (... purpose Eq <urn:purpose/marketing>) unsatisfied`, but the evaluator formats `c.right`, the policy constraint's right operand. For the shown Turtle that operand is `<urn:purpose/research>`, regardless of the request's supplied marketing value. Thus this is not the claimed real deterministic `evaluate()` output. The new shape-only test accepts either value and does not detect the error.
issues.fix_hint: Change the caveat to `rule <urn:rule/use-research> constraint (http://www.w3.org/ns/odrl/2/purpose Eq <urn:purpose/research>) unsatisfied`, then add a fixture/test generated by or asserted against the Rust evaluator for this exact named-rule policy and request so future operand substitutions fail the gate. At minimum, add a JS semantic consistency check that parses/anchors the caveat operand to the policy constraint rather than validating only its regex shape.
issues.title: Purpose-mismatch caveat is not the evaluator's output

summary: Round 2 improves the honesty language and fixes the Request builder rendering, but the walkthrough still publishes at least one value as real evaluator output when it cannot be produced by the shown policy. The purpose-mismatch caveat substitutes the request value (`marketing`) where eval.rs formats the policy constraint's right operand (`research`). The JS shape checks cannot catch this. The prior broad provenance objections are substantially narrowed, but this concrete captured-output error remains release-blocking.
